column3:
Execute a graphics command from the registry.

General form:
DRAW commandName, arg1, arg2, ...

How to write commandName:
1) Bare command: DRAW line, 0, 0, 100, 100
2) Quoted command: DRAW "line", 0, 0, 100, 100
3) Group-qualified command: DRAW "HUD.line", 0, 0, 100, 100
4) Nested groups: DRAW "Ship.Engine.line", 0, 0, 100, 100

Group notation means everything before the last dot is a group path, and the final token is the command.
Example: "Ship.Engine.rect" => group path Ship -> Engine, command rect.

Tip: place cursor on a subcommand token (LINE, RECT, CIRCLE, etc.) for command-specific help.

DRAW line command.

Signature:
line x1 y1 x2 y2

Draw a line segment between two points.

Arguments: exactly 4 numbers.

DRAW rect command.

Signature:
rect x y width height

Draw a rectangle.

Arguments: exactly 4 numbers.

DRAW circle command.

Signature:
circle cx cy radius

Draw a circle from center and radius.

Arguments: exactly 3 numbers.

DRAW ellipse command.

Signature:
ellipse x y width height

Draw an ellipse.

Arguments: exactly 4 numbers.

DRAW stroke command.

Signature:
stroke r g b [a]

Set stroke (outline) color. Alpha is optional.

Arguments: 3 to 4 numbers.

DRAW fill command.

Signature:
fill r g b [a]

Set fill color. Alpha is optional.

Arguments: 3 to 4 numbers.

DRAW noFill command.

Signature:
noFill

Disable fill for subsequent shapes.

Arguments: none.

DRAW noStroke command.

Signature:
noStroke

Disable stroke for subsequent shapes.

Arguments: none.

DRAW lineWidth command.

Signature:
lineWidth width

Set stroke line width.

Arguments: exactly 1 number.

DRAW background command.

Signature:
background r g b [a]

Set canvas background color. Alpha is optional.

Arguments: 3 to 4 numbers.

DRAW clear command.

Signature:
clear

Clear the canvas.

Arguments: none.

DRAW translate command.

Signature:
translate dx dy

Translate a group transform.

Arguments: exactly 2 numbers.

DRAW rotate command.

Signature:
rotate angle

Rotate a group transform in degrees.

Arguments: exactly 1 number.

DRAW scale command.

Signature:
scale sx [sy]

Scale a group transform; sy defaults to sx when omitted.

Arguments: 1 to 2 numbers.

DRAW text command.

Signature:
text x y "text"

Draw text at a position using current stroke color and font size.

Arguments: exactly 2 numeric values plus 1 quoted string.

DRAW fontSize command.

Signature:
fontSize size

Set font size for subsequent DRAW text commands.

Arguments: exactly 1 number.

Play a melody/control sequence string.

Form:
PLAY sequence$ [, options...]

Stop active playback:
PLAY "STOP"

Implemented runtime options:
- ASYNC or NOWAIT
- VOL=<n> or VOL, n
- DUR=<ms> or DUR, ms

Current ABC subset (runtime parseABCToTones):
1) Notes
A B C D E F G

2) Accidentals
^ sharp, _ flat, = natural marker

3) Rests
z or Z
Lengths allowed: z2, z/2, z/

4) Directives
L=<beats> or L:<beats> (default note length)
Q=<bpm> or Q:<bpm>
Q:<unit>=<bpm> supported, e.g. Q:1/4=123

5) Octave modifiers
lowercase note: +1 octave
comma (,): -1 octave each
apostrophe ('): +1 octave each
explicit octave digit overrides shifts, e.g. C5

6) Length suffixes
A2, A/2, A/, A3/2

7) Tokens ignored by parser
bar separators: |, ||, |], [|
comments: % to end of line
ties: -

Examples:
PLAY "L=1/2 Q:120 C z2 | D z/2 || E [| F |]"
PLAY "L=1 Q=120 C D E % major scale F G A B C'"
PLAY "L:1/8 Q:1/4=123 C D E F G A B c"

Notes:
- This is a lightweight subset, not full ABC/MML.
- Engine keeps ABC state (L and Q) across successive PLAY calls until STOP.

Play a sine tone.

Form:
TONE frequency, durationMs

frequency is in Hz and duration is in milliseconds.

Return one segment from a delimited string.

Form:
SPLIT$(text$, delimiter$, index)

index is zero-based in current runtime behavior.

Start a CLOSED PROC asynchronously (fire-and-forget).

Form:
SPAWN procName
SPAWN procName(arg1, arg2, ...)

Rules in current implementation:
- Target must be a PROC (not FUNC).
- Target PROC must be declared CLOSED.
- Spawned workers are detached from the main flow (no process id / no join).
- When the main program ends, spawned workers are stopped.
- A spawned CLOSED PROC can call only CLOSED PROC/FUNC routines.
- DIM/LOCAL is not allowed inside CLOSED PROC/FUNC in this restrictive mode.
